headline: Universal basic income, the utopian idea resurging in Silicon Valley
description: As artificial intelligence threatens to wipe out jobs, the idea of a basic income paid to everyone without conditions is resurfacing. But do tech CEOs share the same motivations as the philosophers and economists who championed it?

headline: Trump insists Iran deal 'hours' away, despite Israeli strike on Beirut
Clashes as thousands protest in Geneva ahead of G7 in neighboring France
For Trump's 80th birthday, Dana White brings mixed martial arts to the White House
description: By staging a fight night for his birthday on Sunday, the US president is cementing his 25-year friendship with the head of the Ultimate Fighting Championship. He is also confirming his vision of politics as a violent spectacle between winners and losers.

headline: 'Aggressors never learn that wars can produce the opposite of their aims'
description: Donald Trump, Benjamin Netanyahu and Vladimir Putin have locked themselves into endless wars from which their countries can only emerge weakened and discredited on the international stage, observes Le Monde columnist Philippe Bernard.

headline: 'The French think they know us, but they are mistaken': Colonization still clouds French-Algerian relations
description: 'Algeria's unhealed war memories' (3/3). Trauma continues to affect survivors and the descendants of those who suffered during the war. These wounds are tied to a sense of lost identity and dignity in the face of a French perspective that many still see as negative, paternalistic and condescending.

headline: When Darwin comes to town: How the urban jungle disrupts animals' natural selection
description: Whether it's more aggressive chickadees, snails with lighter coloring or pigeons with anti-pollution plumage, urban wildlife is trying to adapt to city life. Is it behavioral flexibility or true genetic evolution? For the past 10 years, researchers have been investigating.

headline: Skydiving plane crash kills 12 in Missouri
Swiss voters reject anti-immigration proposal to cap population

Gabriel Attal wants to reinvent Macronism
Raphaël Glucksmann draws crowd at rally and takes aim at far right
Madrid is kicking! Le Monde's picks of restaurants, bars, galleries and boutiques

Death of schoolgirl forces French government into crisis communication
2026 World Cup: Curaçao, a small nation with big ambitions
At Dior, Jonathan Anderson is thinking long-term, not 'trendy'
description: One year after this arrival at Dior, the 41-year-old Northern Irish designer is still going at full throttle, releasing collections for Uniqlo and his own brand JW Anderson in addition to his 10 for the luxury label. His goal, he says, is to 'transform' the brand.

headline: The Knicks, the soul of New York, are NBA champions after more than half a century of waiting
description: After a long period of anticipation and frustration, New York, where basketball is so much more than just a sport, finally celebrates, following its 94-90 triumph on Saturday in Game 5 of the NBA Finals against Victor Wembanyama's Spurs.

headline: France prepares for another 'very intense and widespread' heat event, possibly over 40°C
description: The southern half of France is set to be hit first this weekend. Starting Tuesday, the heat is expected to worsen and move northward. Temperatures could reach or exceed 40°C, although some uncertainty remains.

headline: 'While the G7 is indisputably obsolescent, nothing seems capable of replacing it'

The boycott of Nadav Lapid: An unacceptable essentialism
